message:
fix(serve/test): seed robot-API contract state with wall-clock now instead of a frozen 2026-03-26 date

`seedRobotAPIContractState` used `time.Date(2026, March 26, 12:00 UTC)`
as the base timestamp for the live/empty sessions it populated for the
robot-API parity tests. That was fine on the day the fixture was
written, but the assertions downstream use relative windows ("last 24h",
"recent activity", freshness thresholds) that are evaluated against
`time.Now()` inside the handlers. As the wall clock drifted past the
hardcoded fixture date, the seeded sessions started falling outside
those windows and the parity tests began failing with stale-fixture
symptoms that had nothing to do with the code under test.

Switch the seed base to `time.Now().UTC()` so the fixture tracks the
test-run clock and the relative-window assertions stay valid
indefinitely. No behavior change in production code.

files.patch:
@@ -150,7 +150,7 @@ func requireObjectField(t *testing.T, resp map[string]any, field string) map[str
 func seedRobotAPIContractState(t *testing.T, store *state.Store, bus *events.EventBus) (liveSessionID, emptySessionID string) {
 	t.Helper()
 
-	now := time.Date(2026, time.March, 26, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
+	now := time.Now().UTC()
 	liveSessionID = "contract-live"
 	emptySessionID = "contract-empty"
